summary: Verified real-time sync fix with polling fallback. All features working correctly: piece movement via drag-and-drop, SYNC indicator shows when polling mode active, game state syncs on refresh, timer behavior correct, move API works, admin analyze player endpoint works, tournament scheduler running.
passed_tests: Piece movement via drag-and-drop works (tested g1f3 move)
Move is sent to server and persisted in database
Real-time sync via polling works (~1 second delay)
SYNC indicator (gold) shows when polling mode is active
Game state syncs correctly on page refresh
Timer behavior correct - white timer starts first
Tournament scheduler is running (automated tournaments created)
critical_code_review_comments: WebSocket connection fails at proxy level (expected behavior) - polling fallback works correctly
Console shows 'Socket connection error: server error' but this is handled gracefully with polling
Polling interval is 1 second which provides acceptable real-time experience for rapid/classical games
seed_data_creation: Used test game game_ac1776b92f82 - now has 3 moves (e2e4, e7e5, g1f3)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Real-time sync is working via polling fallback (1 second interval). WebSocket fails at proxy level but polling provides acceptable latency. Test game game_ac1776b92f82 has 3 moves and is active with black to move. All features verified working.

verified_features.real_time_sync: WORKING - polling fallback active when WebSocket fails
verified_features.sync_indicator: WORKING - gold SYNC indicator shows polling mode
verified_features.game_state_refresh: WORKING - state syncs on page load

mocked_apis.wallet_system: Cryptocurrency wallet balances are SIMULATED - no real blockchain integration
